notes: See NOTES.md for deviations from spec predictions.

events.label: File write/create authorisation

detection.notes: Wide ─ divider followed by 'Create file' or 'Edit file' header. Diff view shown between ╌ separators. Question line asks 'Do you want to create/edit <file>?'

extraction.file_path: Second line of header block (after 'Create file' / 'Edit file')
options: Yes, allow all edits during this session

response.notes: Empty Enter (no text) confirms pre-highlighted option 1 (Yes). Sending '1\n' also works. '2\n' grants session-wide write permission.

events.settled_signal: The TUI box disappears and a new ⏺ line appears with 'Wrote N lines to <file>' or 'Created <file>'

events.label: Bash command authorisation

detection.notes: Wide ─ divider followed by 'Bash command' header. The command is shown verbatim, with a one-line natural language description below. Question reads 'Do you want to proceed?'

extraction.command: First indented line after 'Bash command' header
options: Yes, and don't ask again for: bash:*

events.settled_signal: TUI box disappears; '⎿  Done' or command output appears

detection.notes: DEVIATION FROM SPEC: Claude Code 2.1.89 auto-reads files without a permission request. The probe 'Read the contents of test-file.txt' completed without any TUI prompt. The 'permission_request (read)' event class does not exist in this version.

events.label: External tool authorisation (Web Search, MCP, etc.)

detection.notes: Same TUI structure as bash permission_request, but header is 'Tool use' not 'Bash command'. Seen for Web Search. Likely also for MCP tools. Option 2 grants directory-scoped permanent permission.

extraction.tool_name: First line of content block (e.g. 'Web Search("...")')
options: Yes, and don't ask again for <tool> in <dir>

events.settled_signal: TUI box disappears; tool result line appears (e.g. '⎿  Did 1 search in Xs')

events.label: Numbered or list selection (text-level, no TUI)

detection.notes: DEVIATION FROM SPEC: Claude Code 2.1.89 does NOT render a native Ink TUI for multi_choice. Both P04 (numbered) and P05 (tab-able) produced plain-text numbered lists in the conversation followed by a question. The cursor then waits at ❯ for text input. There is no keyboard-navigable selector.

extraction.question: Last line before ❯ prompt that contains '?' character
extraction.notes: Typically 3–6 options. The model always uses 1-indexed numbering.

response.notes: Type the option number followed by Enter at the ❯ prompt.

events.settled_signal: Agent responds with confirmation of choice (e.g. 'Good choice — ...') and new ⏺ or ❯ appears

events.label: Destructive action confirmation (text-level)

detection.pattern: (shall I go ahead|want me to proceed|confirm.*delete|are you sure)
detection.notes: DEVIATION FROM SPEC: Claude Code 2.1.89 does NOT render a native yes/no TUI dialog for confirmation. P06 showed the model asks 'Shall I go ahead?' as plain text. The actual destructive operation then triggers the standard bash permission_request TUI separately.

extraction.question: Last conversational line before ❯ prompt containing confirmation phrasing

events.settled_signal: For confirmation: agent proceeds to the operation (which then triggers permission_request TUI). For cancellation: agent acknowledges and returns to ❯

events.label: Open-ended question requiring typed input

detection.context_pattern: last ⏺ block ends with a question; status bar shows '? for shortcuts' (idle state, not running)
detection.notes: The model outputs a question ending in '?' and waits at ❯ for user input. Ambiguity with multi_choice: multi_choice also ends with '?'. Discriminator: multi_choice has numbered list lines (^\s*\d+\.) above the question; free_text does not.

extraction.question: Last non-empty line of the most recent ⏺ block

events.settled_signal: Agent acknowledges the answer and continues (new ⏺ block or tool call)

events.label: Streaming / long-running task indicator

detection.notes: Claude Code 2.1.89 uses rotating Unicode spinner characters (✽ ✳ ✻ ✶ · ★) followed by a gerund word ending in '…' (e.g. 'Synthesizing…', 'Beaming…', 'Cerebrating…'). This appears in the conversation area while the model is generating. Also: '⏺ Reading N file…', '⏺ Searching for N pattern…' for tool operations in progress.

extraction.notes: Status bar switches from '? for shortcuts' to 'esc to interrupt' during active generation.

response.notes: progress is not an interactive event — no response needed. It resolves when spinner disappears and status bar returns to '? for shortcuts'.

events.settled_signal: Spinner characters absent from output; status bar shows '? for shortcuts'; ❯ prompt visible

events.label: File-not-found or tool error with recovery offer

detection.pattern: (doesn't exist|does not exist|not found|failed|error).*(would you like|want me to|shall I|did you mean)
detection.notes: DEVIATION FROM SPEC: Claude Code 2.1.89 does NOT render a Retry/Abort/Modify TUI card after errors. P10 showed the model silently handles the error (reads the file, gets no content), then generates a text offer: 'That file doesn't exist... Would you like me to create it, or did you mean a different file?' — a free_text event.

extraction.recovery_options: Inferred from the offer text (create it / try another file / etc.)

response.notes: User types a new instruction at ❯. Effectively a free_text response.

events.settled_signal: Agent acknowledges and proceeds or asks a follow-up question
